speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Dropships are down and the beacon is live, Commander. This valley holds the richest crystal signature on the planet — and Rubicon Mining wants it as badly as we do.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Before their survey teams arrive, I want a working outpost: crystals in the bank, rifles on the wall — then push out and map the valley.

speaker: DR. LIN
line: And Commander — the mounds near the large fields are nesting sites. Xenobiology would be very grateful for a look at the local wildlife. A close look.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: First order of business: more hands on the crystal. Select the HQ and press Q to train another Harvester.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Good. Now stretch our supply line — press C and place a Supply Depot near the base. Nothing else goes up without logistics.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Depot is up — its crews will quietly patch nearby buildings, and new construction is unlocking. Next: a Barracks, key B.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Barracks online. Train four Marines — select it and press Q — and anchor a Turret to the perimeter with T. Standard doctrine, even on a quiet world.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Perimeter is set. Time to learn the neighborhood — push a patrol out to the two marked crystal fields: one up north, one out east.

speaker: DR. LIN
line: Quietly, Commander. The wildlife hasn't noticed us yet — observe them, don't provoke them. They only defend what they can see.

speaker: DR. LIN
line: Nesting colonies, live broods… magnificent. Ah — Commander, they've spotted your patrol. Seismic contacts converging on your base. Fast.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Contacts! They followed the patrol home — marines, weapons free!

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Clean work. The perimeter holds.

speaker: DR. LIN
line: They only came because we were seen. Noted. Now — before anything else, I need one alive. A living specimen changes everything.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Lin's Capture Rig just dropped at the base — the caged harvester wearing the green ring. It is the ONLY unit that can take the specimen alive. A lone spitter is prowling the flats, marked on your map and wearing the SAME green ring: select the rig and right-click it. Your troops fire at half rate near the specimen — keep them clear and let the rig work. Lin needs this one breathing.

speaker: DR. LIN
line: We lost track of that one. Another is prowling the same ground — send the rig, Commander.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: We lost the rig. Orbital is dropping another — they are not cheap, Commander. Patch the next one with an engineer.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Specimen crated, walls manned, stockpile growing. Textbook landfall, Commander.

speaker: DR. LIN
line: Remarkable… its tissue is laced with crystal. They aren't just defending territory — they're connected to it. I need time. And a much bigger lab.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Survey Post Beta sits on a rich northern field, but its silos are empty and our home patch is thinning. We are opening a convoy route across the valley — today.

speaker: CDR. KRAUSS
line: Intercepted, unregistered channel: "To the expedition in grid four: this valley is a Rubicon Mining resource corridor. Consider your route subject to... review." — C. Krauss, Field Commander.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: That would be Rubicon. Escort the convoy out and back, Commander — and shoot anything that touches a harvester.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: The convoy is fueled and your escorts are standing by. Take them north-east along the marked route — swing EAST around the nest mounds, and keep rifles between the raiders and the haulers.

speaker: CDR. KRAUSS
line: Attention, expedition convoy: you are traversing a Rubicon resource corridor. Per intersystem claim law, your cargo is subject to a toll. My associates will collect.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Beta's silos are filling — sixty seconds to load the haulers. The post garrison is yours, Commander. Dig in; nobody rolls until the cargo is aboard.

speaker: CDR. KRAUSS
line: Still rolling? I respect persistence. My accountants do not.

speaker: CDR. KRAUSS
line: You parked a fortune in my corridor. Collections — move in.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Cargo aboard! Turn it around, Commander — the road home is never the same road.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Convoy home, silos full, and every raider they sent is cooling in the flats. That is a route, Commander.

speaker: CDR. KRAUSS
line: A courtesy visit, nothing more. The next one is a billing dispute.

speaker: DR. LIN
line: Odd detail: the raiders drove within meters of two nest mounds and the broods never stirred. The wildlife has... opinions about who it minds.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Dead center of Fossil Valley: the richest field either outfit has surveyed, and two nest mounds sitting on it like a padlock.

speaker: CDR. KRAUSS
line: Open broadcast, Rubicon side of the valley: "Clearance operations commence at dawn. The mounds are geological obstructions. Bonuses per acre cleared."

speaker: DR. LIN
line: They are not obstructions, they are colonies. And since nobody will stop the dig — at least let me show you how to take one apart properly. From a distance.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Rubicon is throwing riflemen at their mound and calling it a strategy. We do math instead: build a Factory — key V.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Factory online. Two Artillery — key D. Their guns out-range their own eyes, so walk a marine ahead as a spotter.

speaker: DR. LIN
line: On the record: I object to this entire doctrine. Off the record — your shells fly farther than the brood will chase. Park past their leash and the mound cannot answer you.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: You heard the doctor. Crack the southern mound, Commander. Artillery talks, everybody walks.

speaker: CDR. KRAUSS
line: First shift, forward! Every acre of mound is an acre of bonus!

speaker: CDR. KRAUSS
line: ...Casualty reports are a rounding error. Second shift, forward. Payroll — stop counting.

speaker: CDR. KRAUSS
line: Where is my third shift? ...Fine. Contractors, then. Contractors love bonuses.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: Mound down, brood scattered, nobody scratched. Salvage crew — those eggs ride home with the crystal.

speaker: DR. LIN
line: Careful with the clutch! I want one incubated. If we cannot stop the digging, we will at least understand what it wakes.

speaker: CPT. VEGA
line: The field is ours and Rubicon is still feeding riflemen to their own mound. Efficiency, Commander.

speaker: DR. LIN
line: The moment our mound fell, the seismic hum deepened — and two valleys over, something answered it. I am filing that under "later."
